data.title: US Trade Defict Falls By 24% In August As Tariffs Lowers Imports
data.description:
The US trade deficit fell by nearly 24% in August as President Trump’s tariffs pushed imports lower, but trade could still subtract from economic growth in ‌Q3.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/special-reports/us-trade-defict-falls-by-24-in-august-as-tariffs-lowers-imports/

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Now is the time to buy US stocks; the technical correction may have ended.
data.description: JPMorgan's trading division said the longest losing streak for U.S. stocks since August has created opportunities for bargain hunters. The S&P 500 fell for four consecutive days, accumulating a 3.4% drop by Tuesday's close, as investors worried about the sustainability of the artificial intelligence rally and the Federal Reserve's monetary policy path. Andrew Tyler, JPMorgan's global head of market intelligence, said this pullback represents a "technical shakeout" in the stock market, and the correction period may have ended. "Given that there have been no changes in the fundamentals, and our investment assumptions do not rely on the Fed easing policy, now is the time to buy on the dips," Tyler wrote in a report to clients on Wednesday. (Jinshi)

data.title: The plunge in Trump Media's stock price has wiped out more than $5 billion of the Trump family's wealth.
data.description: Shares of cryptocurrency and social media company Trump Media & Technology Group have plummeted, falling nearly 70% this year, including a 34.6% drop last month alone. Coupled with the sluggish cryptocurrency market, the Trump family's wealth has evaporated by more than $5 billion. It is reported that Trump himself indirectly holds approximately 115 million shares in the company. (NYPost)

data.title: Bitwise CEO Predicts Surge in Crypto ETFs
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley has forecasted a significant expansion in the crypto ETF market, anticipating the launch of over 100 products. He expects a substantial increase in single-asset crypto exchange-traded products (ETPs), with particular excitement surrounding the growth of index-based crypto ETPs.

Despite challenging market conditions, Horsley believes that index-based ETPs will become one of the most significant narratives in the crypto sector next year, eventually emerging as a major category for investors.

Bitwise's Matt Hougan added that the industry is poised to grow tenfold in the future. On October 28, Bitwise introduced a Solana staking ETF, which tracks the price of the cryptocurrency Solana.

data.title: Analysis: Since 2018, Bitcoin has experienced a significant rebound after each major price drop.
data.description: Economist Alex Krüger, writing on the X platform, points out that since 2018, Bitcoin has experienced 11 crashes. While short-term weakness is common after each crash, a rebound has followed each time. This pattern is one of Bitcoin's most reliable behavioral advantages: when panic reaches its peak, expected returns tend to rise sharply. In past cycles, whenever the cryptocurrency fear and greed index fell to 10 or below, Bitcoin consistently delivered strong expected returns.

data.title: Fed Policy Uncertainty Heightened by Data Fog, Officials Cautious on Rate Cuts
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the uncertainty surrounding Federal Reserve policy has intensified due to concerns about inflation persistence among several Fed officials, who remain cautious about further rate cuts. This has kept U.S. Treasury yields within a relatively narrow range as traders await new catalysts to alter market pricing.

Michael Lorizio, Head of U.S. Rates and Mortgage Trading at Manulife Investment Management, noted the prolonged absence of top-tier economic data guidance, making it challenging to form a confident assessment of the current state of the U.S. economy before new data emerges.

The upcoming release of the Federal Reserve's October meeting minutes may provide further insight into how many policymakers might oppose a rate cut in December. "When there seemed to be many differing opinions previously, obtaining summary information through the meeting minutes today may help further shape market expectations for the December meeting," Lorizio stated.

data.title: Kraken exchange files for US IPO
data.description:
The 13-year-old exchange has submitted a draft registration statement as it prepares for a public listing. On Nov. 19, Payward Inc. , the operator of the crypto exchange, announced that it has confidentially submitted a Form S-1 draft to the U. S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for a proposed initial public offering of its common stock. […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/kraken-exchange-files-for-us-ipo/

data.title: Cardano Teeters on Key Support as Whale Shock Drags ADA Toward Multi-Month Lows — Here Is Why the Market Is Bracing for $0.43
data.description:
Cardano is currently precariously positioned at a crucial support level, as significant selling pressure from large investors, often referred to as "whales," has caused its native cryptocurrency, ADA, to approach levels not seen in several months. This alarming trend has led market analysts to speculate that the price could potentially drop to as low as $0.43. Understanding the underlying factors contributing to this situation is vital, as it sheds light on why both investors and traders are preparing for possible further declines in the market.
source: https://blocknews.com/cardano-teeters-on-key-support-as-whale-shock-drags-ada-toward-multi-month-lows-here-is-why-the-market-is-bracing-for-0-43/

data.title: The divergence in the Fed's interest rate cut path has widened, and the meeting minutes are expected to clarify the direction.
data.description: The data fog has exacerbated uncertainty surrounding Federal Reserve policy, as many Fed officials expressed concerns about sticky inflation and remained cautious about further rate cuts. This has also kept U.S. Treasury yields within a relatively narrow range, as traders await new catalysts to change market pricing. “We’ve lacked guidance from top-tier economic data for far too long, and it’s difficult to form a highly confident judgment about the current state of the U.S. economy until we start seeing the data,” said Michael Lorizio, head of U.S. interest rates and mortgage trading at Manulife Investment Management. The upcoming minutes of the Fed’s October meeting may further reveal how many policymakers might oppose a rate cut in December. “When there seemed to be a lot of disagreement before, the summary information from today’s minutes could help further shape market expectations for the December meeting,” Lorizio said. (Jinshi)

data.title: ETH Holdings Face Liquidation Challenges
data.description: According to Odaily, blockchain analyst Yu Jin reports that investor Ma Ji Da Ge has faced liquidation of his Ethereum (ETH) positions. Earlier today, he increased his ETH holdings, but shortly thereafter, 20% of his position, approximately 775 ETH, was liquidated. Currently, he retains a position of 2,480 ETH.

data.title: In the past four hours, a total of $111 million in positions were liquidated across the entire network, of which approximately $37.48 million was in Ethereum.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $111 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past 4 hours, mainly long positions. Among them, ETH liquidations amounted to approximately $37.48 million and BTC liquidations amounted to approximately $29.47 million.
